items.caption:
and a whole lot happier 🥹🩷

#weightloss#weight#weightlossstory#viral#girls

I realised it in the middle of the night,
sitting on the edge of the bed,
knowing there was no one else to tap in.

No one coming to take over.
No one to split the weight with.

There’s this idea that you’re supposed to “hold it together.”
Like life is something you stabilise if you try hard enough.

But doing this alone isn’t about holding it together.
It’s about absorbing impact.

Everything lands with you.
The bills.
The decisions.
The worry.
The consequences.

Even the small things carry weight when there’s no backup.

If they’re sick, it’s you.
If money is tight, it’s you.
If something goes wrong, it’s you figuring it out before morning.

And the nights…
the nights stretch longer when there’s no one beside you
to share the silence or the fear.

It’s not just loneliness.
It’s responsibility with no pause button.

You start measuring everything differently.

Not by what you want
but by what you can carry without everything else collapsing.

Some days you give them the best of you
and take what’s left for yourself.

Other days, there isn’t a “best.”
Just enough to get through.

And the guilt finds its way in anyway.
For being tired.
For being short.
For not being more than one person.

But this is the part no one really names—

when you’re a single mum,
there’s no clean version of doing it right.

There’s just the quiet, constant calculation
of what you can survive carrying
and what you have to let go
even when it matters.

#weight #parenting#solomum#singlemum

💥 It’s no secret that diet is one of the biggest keys when it comes to seeing results from all your hard work 🔥💪🏾 

💥 You can train every single day and still not see progress if you constantly overeat calories, fats, or carbs. Training is important — but nutrition really matters if your goal is fat loss and building muscle 💪

💥 What I eat changes from week to week and month to month depending on my schedule, stress levels, goals, and how much time I have. But I always try to stay within the boundaries that support the results I want.

💥So today I wanted to share what I ate yesterday 👇
Breakfast:
3 egg omelette, 45g bread & cucumber 🥚🥒
Lunch:
130g chicken breast & 90g cooked rice 🍗🍚
Dinner:
2 burger patties, 45g bread & a side salad 🍔🥗

.
💥 + 2 snacks 👀

💥💥 Comment below if you want me to show some of my favorite high protein snacks too ✨
FULL meal plans, recipes & more meal ideas are available through the link in my bio on my training app 📲

The 4 things I’d tell you if you were my bestie trying to lose weight 

- I stopped hating my body
absolutely no negative self talk! allowed
I wore clothes & purchased pants that fit NOW. I chose a mantra that had nothing to do with my body “I keep promises to myself”. 

- I made it EASY on myself 
only workout 3 days week
roll out of bed in PJs for your workout
still eat foods I enjoy during the week (sourdough for breakfast every day yes plz)
didn’t feel like working out? go for a walk

- I shifted my treats to the weekend
the week was a safe zone. whole (still YUMMY) nutrient dense foods. I had ice cream, pizza & Diet Coke every weekend. this fave me something to look forward and a boundary to stick to. 

- I started WALKING with a vest.
for the love of all things, start this habit. you will feel strong, you will have energy, your mental health will feel 1000x stronger, and hi, you’ll probably lose weight without even thinking about it. 

3 years later I’m 60lbs down.

. this is why I made the RAISE HELL HEAL WELL method. so you can have an exact framework for yourself to:

— feel amazing in your skin
— feel confident in your clothes again
— lose weight 
— create healthy habits
— get results without hating yourself
— drop lbs without hating your life 
— figure out why you self sabotage 
— learn how to make your weight loss STICK

this isn’t a quick fix. it’s a commitment to lifelong change. you will get nowhere until you start — and there’s absolutely no reason why you can’t start TODAY. 

❤️‍🔥when you’re ready to lose weight without all the complicated hAcKs comment “READY” & I’ll message you a link to grab the exact method I created and used to lose 60lbs naturally

#weightloss #weightlosstips #weightlossjourney #beforeandafter #beforandafterweightloss

High protein creamy potato salad 🥔✨

Potato salad doesn’t need to be drowning in mayo to taste good. This version is creamy, filling, packed with protein and honestly good enough to eat on its own.

✨ Ingredients:
* 200g Carisma potatoes
* 1 egg
* 15g light mayonnaise
* 2 slices shortcut bacon (cut off the fat and rind)
* 100g chicken breast (raw weight)
* 25g red onion
* ½ cup spring onion
* 40g natural light yoghurt

✨ Macros:
Calories: ~451 (slightly less without the bacon fat and rind!)
Protein: ~45g
Carbs: ~28g
Fat: ~17g

This is one of those meals that feels indulgent while still helping you hit your goals 🤝

A tale of two lifestyles…

One picture is me out partying. The other is me sitting in a sauna, investing in my health and recovery 🔥

And honestly… years ago I never thought I’d become the guy choosing saunas, recovery and early mornings over 4am finishes and hangovers 😂

I started raving back in the acid house days and partied heavily well into my late 30s. Trust me, there’s not much you can tell me about late nights, festivals, alcohol or substances. I fully lived that life.

But …..eventually……it catches up with you.

What most people don’t realise is you can’t keep taking from your body without eventually paying the price.

The lack of sleep. The stress. The alcohol. The poor nutrition. The constant “I’ll sort myself out next week.”

Over time it starts showing up as:

Low energy
Weight gain
High blood pressure
Anxiety & brain fog
Poor sleep
Aching joints
Feeling older than your actual age
Increased risk of heart attack & stroke

That’s why I changed direction.

Not because I wanted to look good for Instagram… but because I wanted to genuinely FEEL good again.

Now my focus is different:

🏋🏾‍♂️Training
🚶🏾‍♂️Walking
🥗 Eating better
💧 Drinking more water
🔥 Sauna & recovery
😴 Better sleep

Because the real flex nowadays isn’t partying all weekend…

It’s having the energy to play with your kids.To feel confident in your body again.To wake up without feeling broken.To still feel strong and healthy 10–20 years from now.

That’s why I do what I do 🙏🏾

Seeing people become healthier, happier and more confident genuinely gives me purpose, because I know how life-changing it feels when you finally start looking after yourself properly.

If you’re at that crossroads and know it’s time to invest in your future self, get in touch.

🥊 Boxercise & Sauna Event📅 30th May📍 Community Sauna, Ruskin Park SE5💷 £25

A chance to train, reset mentally, recover and be around good people with good energy.

Comment RESET if you’re ready to start putting you

Я знаю, что сейчас многие напишут: 
«Невозможно худеть в профиците калорий».

Но тогда объясните мне, почему мои пациентки годами не могли похудеть на 1200–1500 калорий, ходили голодные, срывались, теряли волосы, цикл и энергию, а потом переходили на низкоуглеводное питание или карнивор, начинали есть 2000–3000 калорий и худели?

И нет, они не начинали внезапно тренироваться по 5 часов в день.

Проблема в том, что человеческий организм не калькулятор. 
На вес влияет не только количество калорий, но и гормоны, инсулин, уровень воспаления, сытость, аппетит, удержание воды, уровень стресса, качество сна и метаболическая адаптация.

Два одинаковых количества калорий могут давать абсолютно разный гормональный ответ.

Когда человек постоянно голодный, с инсулиновыми качелями, воспалением и тягой к еде, организм работает иначе.

В реальной практике всё намного сложнее, чем формула «меньше ешь и больше худей».

Bulking vs cutting isn’t about “good” vs “bad” foods—it’s about how you use them.

Protein, carbs, and fats all play a role. The difference comes down to quantity, quality, and intention.

On a bulk, you’re fueling growth. That means more calories, more energy, and foods that are easier to eat in larger amounts.

On a cut, you’re focusing on efficiency. Lean proteins, controlled portions, and foods that keep you full without pushing calories too high.

Same categories. Different strategy.

Once you understand that, nutrition stops being confusing—and starts becoming something you can actually control.

Follow me for daily health tips and save this video for later.

#fitness #nutrition #healthylifestyle #weightloss #fyp

Hope you also like some Ateez playing in the background 😈

💭 What is your favorite ab exercise???

#absworkout #homeworkouts #weightloss #kpop

CREAMY GARLIC BEEF & MUSHROOM PASTA 🥩🍄🍝
(Makes 6 Servings: Each Serving is 1/6 of the Pan)

Macros (Per Serving):
-557 Calories
-17g Fat
-45g Carbs
-56g Protein

📘 Comment “COOKBOOK” and I will DM you a link to my Macro Friendly Cookbook with 280+ recipes just like this one and FREE LIFETIME UPDATES! (Or Link in bio 🔗)

Ingredients:
-2 lbs 96/4 Lean Ground Beef (907g raw)
-1: 12 oz Box Barilla Protein+ Pasta (340g dry)
-1 Onion, Diced
-12 oz Mushrooms, Sliced
-2 Cups Baby Spinach (60g)
-3 Tbsp Minced Garlic (45g)
-1 Tbsp Tomato Paste (16g)
-1 Cup Beef Broth (240mL)
-2 Cups Parmesan Cheese (200g)
-1 tsp Salt
-1/2 tsp Black Pepper
-1 tsp Italian Seasoning

Instructions:
1.) Cook pasta, drain, and set aside.
2.) Cook beef, onion, and seasonings until browned.
3.) Add mushrooms and cook until softened.
4.) Stir in garlic and tomato paste.
5.) Add broth and parmesan; simmer until slightly thickened.
6.) Add pasta and spinach; mix until wilted and coated.
7.) Divide into 6 servings and store or serve.

Enjoy!

หลังจากตั้งใจดูแลตัวเองมาตลอด 7 เดือน, แต่ก็รู้สึกว่าตัวเองมาได้ไกลมากเหมือนกัน เป้าเราคือ 20% Bodyfat ภายใน 1 ปีอีก5เดือน ไปกานต่อ🥳

#weightloss

LAZY LASAGNA BAKE 🍝
(Makes 6 Servings: Each Serving is ~2.25 Cups or 1/6 of the Casserole)

Macros (Per Serving):
-554 Calories
-18g Fat
-46g Carbs
-52g Protein

📘 Comment “COOKBOOK” and I will DM you a link to my Macro Friendly Cookbook with 280+ recipes just like this one and FREE LIFETIME UPDATES! (Or Link in bio 🔗)

Ingredients:
-2 lbs Lean Ground Beef (96/4) (908g)
-1 Jar Marinara Sauce (24–25 oz / ~700g)
-1 1/4 Cups Water (300mL)
-10 Oven-Ready Lasagna Noodles (~250g)
-1 Cup Low-Fat Ricotta Cheese (250g)
-2 Cups Part-Skim Mozzarella Cheese, Shredded (224g)
-1 Cup Frozen Spinach (100g)
-1 tsp Italian Seasoning
-1/2 tsp Garlic Powder
-1/2 tsp Salt
-Black Pepper to taste

Instructions:
1.) Preheat oven to 375°F. Brown ground beef 6–8 mins, season, and drain if needed.
2.) In a 9x13 dish, mix marinara, water, ricotta, and seasonings. Add broken noodles, beef, spinach, and half the mozzarella. Stir and press noodles under liquid.
3.) Top with remaining cheese, cover, and bake 45–50 mins. Uncover and bake 10–15 mins until browned.
4.) Rest 10 mins, then divide into 6 servings.

Enjoy!
